preview_text:
The model favors Belgium to win at 38%, but assigns it no decisive advantage. Iran's win probability sits at 30%, only 8 percentage points lower, positioning this as a competitive match rather than a mismatch. Belgium's probability exceeds the draw at 31%, yet the margin is modest enough to suggest genuine uncertainty about the outcome.

The narrow spread across all three outcomes reflects a closely balanced contest. A draw ranks as the second-likeliest result, reflecting structural parity in the model's assessment. No single outcome commands majority probability, indicating the match could plausibly unfold in multiple ways. The competitive nature of these numbers suggests neither team enters with a commanding statistical edge.

The model and market diverge sharply on Belgium's prospects. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both place Belgium's win probability between 67–70%, a gap of roughly 30 percentage points above the model's 38%. Both betting venues assign Iran substantially lower odds—around 11%—compared to the model's 30%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; such divergences warrant review of assumptions on both sides before wagering.
